falsification_conditions.condition: S&P 500 closes above 5,600 on any day in the next 14 days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: A new all-time high would invalidate the 'bottom' pattern match, as the August 2024 bottom was followed by a grind higher, not an immediate blowoff. This would signal the pattern has broken bullish rather than consolidating for a potential breakdown.

falsification_conditions.condition: VIX closes below 12.00 for 3 consecutive days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: Sustained sub-12 VIX indicates complacency and risk-on positioning that is inconsistent with a bottoming environment. The August 2024 bottom saw VIX spike above 38; persistent calm would prove the 'bottom' signal was a false read and the market is in a different regime.

falsification_conditions.condition: 10-year Treasury yield drops below 3.80% and holds there for 5 consecutive days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: A sustained rally in bonds to sub-3.80% would indicate a flight-to-safety or recession pricing that contradicts a 'bottom' call. The August 2024 bottom occurred with yields near 3.85% but quickly reversed higher; a sustained drop would signal this is not a similar bottom but rather a breakdown into a deflationary/recessionary episode.

doctrine: If you can't list what would prove your call wrong, you don't have a real call.
